mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months signals rising inflation expectations, leading to a decrease in the purchasing power of consumers and a subsequent decrease in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over the past 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to an increase in wages and a subsequent increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over the past 6 months signals an increase in supply chain activity, leading to an increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) over the past quarter signals an increase in aggregate demand, leading to an increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in increasing demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) over the past month signals a tightening monetary cycle, leading to a decrease in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Housing Starts (HOUST) over the past quarter signals an increase in housing market activity, leading to an increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in increasing housing market activity periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) and a 1% increase in the Nonfarm Payrolls (PAYEMS) over the past 3 months signal a tightening labor market, leading to an increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
